facts.text: Louisville was founded in 1778 by George Rogers Clark and named after King Louis XVI of France, whose troops aided the American Revolution.
Near Bowman Field, Louisville hosts two Orthodox synagogues, a Jewish Community Center, and affordable housing, reflecting a long‑standing Jewish presence.
The Filson Historical Society, founded in 1884, houses over 1.5 million manuscripts and 50,000 volumes, preserving Louisville’s rich past.
The Louisville Orchestra, established in 1937 by conductor Robert Whitney, has become a cornerstone of the city’s classical music scene.
During World War II, Louisville’s factories produced C‑46 Commando cargo planes, later sold to International Harvester for tractor manufacturing.
